name: Noodles in Sesame Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 Small Asian chilli peppers, seeded and minced
½ cup Chicken stock or broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook noodles until al dente. Drain and rinse in cool water, then drain well.
2. Sprinkle with vegetable oil (or peanut/sesame oil). Store refrigerated for up to several days.
3. To make the sauce: In a small skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add onions, garlic, ginger, and chili peppers. Cook until garlic is soft but not brown. Turn off heat.
4. Add vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, sesame paste, and chicken stock. Heat briefly to combine all ingredients.
5. Stir in sesame oil and let the sauce cool to room temperature.
6. Pour sauce over chilled noodles and mix well.
7. Heap noodles onto a serving platter and garnish with sesame seeds, coriander leaves, and julienned vegetables.
